data.narrative:
Morpheus Space is a Series A space & aerospace company founded by Daniel Bock and István Lőrincz that has raised $43M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 5 public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers, with particular relevance to 4 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: Aalyria ($1.3B Series B), ClearSpace ($400M Series A), Hubble Network ($400M Series B), and Sift ($274M Series B).

Two triangulation methods apply. Stage-progression, using the $43M total raised as a value proxy and applying a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier from Series A to next stage, implies $86M. Peer-median valuation, computed across the 4 comparables (range $274M–$1.3B), implies $400M. A third method—public-comp anchoring using the sector P/S median of 49.8x across public peers—cannot be applied without the target's revenue figures.

The two applicable methods produce a triangulated valuation range of $73M (low) to $460M (high), with a median of $243M. The wide spread reflects material stage and scope heterogeneity within the peer set; ClearSpace, at the same Series A stage, trades at $400M, while earlier-stage valuation dynamics and product-market maturity remain key uncertainties. A post-Series A valuation in the $150M–$350M range would sit midway between stage-progression and peer-median anchors, though ultimate pricing will depend on capital efficiency, tech differentiation, and customer traction relative to the peer cohort.
